req.title: Submit leave request — variant 2
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three equivalence partitions for leave duration: 1-30 days (valid, no extra approval), >30 days without hr-admin (invalid, 422), and the boundary at exactly 30 days. Additional error paths cover date inversion (400) and overlap (409). Idempotency under retry is a distinct positive variant. Tests are distributed across EP, BVA, NEG, DT, and STC heuristics to achieve clause-level coverage of all stated conditions.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
SMTP stub is active for email capture
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with start_date=next Monday, end_date=next Monday+9 business days (10 days)
Assert HTTP 201 and capture request_id
GET /leave-requests/{request_id} and assert status=pending
Assert manager notification record exists
Assert notification email captured by SMTP stub
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_mid_range(auth_session, smtp_stub, clean_leave_state):
    start, end = <next_business_day>(offset=1), <next_business_day>(offset=10)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    request_id = resp.json()["request_id"]
    assert request_id

    detail = auth_session.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}").json()
    assert detail["status"] == "pending"

    notifications = auth_session.get("/notifications", params={"ref": request_id}).json()
    assert any(n["type"] == "manager_notify" for n in notifications)

    emails = smtp_stub.messages(to=<employee_email>)
    assert any(request_id in m["body"] for m in emails)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with exactly 30 business days span
Assert HTTP 201 (no hr-admin approval required at boundary)
Assert response contains request_id and status=pending
trace: R-WEB-058: upper inclusive boundary 30 business days
R-WEB-058: requests <=30 days do NOT require hr-admin approval
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_boundary(auth_session, clean_leave_state):
    start = <next_business_day>(offset=1)
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 29)  # start + 29 more = 30 inclusive
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201

    body = resp.json()
    assert body["request_id"]
    assert body.get("requires_hr_admin") is not True

    detail = auth_session.get(f"/leave-requests/{body['request_id']}").json()
    assert detail["status"] == "pending"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_31_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
User does NOT have hr-admin role
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with 31 business days span
Assert HTTP 422
Assert error code indicates hr-admin approval required
expected: Error payload references hr-admin approval requirement
trace: R-WEB-058: error pathway - requests >30 days without hr-admin returns 422
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_31_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422(auth_session, clean_leave_state):
    start = <next_business_day>(offset=1)
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 30)  # 31 inclusive business days
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422

    error = resp.json()
    assert "hr-admin" in error.get("detail", "").lower() or \
           error.get("error_code") == "HR_ADMIN_APPROVAL_REQUIRED"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_inverted_dates_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with start_date after end_date
Assert HTTP 400
Assert error message references date ordering
expected: Error payload indicates start_date must not exceed end_date
trace: R-WEB-058: error pathway - 400 when start_date > end_date
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_inverted_dates_returns_400(auth_session):
    start = <next_business_day>(offset=5)
    end = <next_business_day>(offset=1)  # end before start
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400

    error = resp.json()
    detail = error.get("detail", "").lower()
    assert "start_date" in detail or "end_date" in detail or \
           error.get("error_code") == "INVALID_DATE_RANGE"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
An existing approved leave exists for next Monday to next Monday+4
actions: Seed an existing leave covering next Monday through next Monday+4
POST /leave-requests/new with overlapping range next Monday+2 to next Monday+7
Assert HTTP 409
expected: Error payload references overlapping leave period
trace: R-WEB-058: error pathway - 409 when overlapping existing leave
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409(auth_session, seed_leave):
    base = <next_business_day>(offset=1)
    seed_leave(start=base, end=<add_business_days>(base, 4))  # existing: days 1-5

    overlap_start = <add_business_days>(base, 2)  # overlaps day 3
    overlap_end = <add_business_days>(base, 7)
    payload = {"start_date": overlap_start, "end_date": overlap_end, "type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409

    error = resp.json()
    assert "overlap" in error.get("detail", "").lower() or \
           error.get("error_code") == "LEAVE_OVERLAP"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: User is authenticated as full_time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Client sends a stable idempotency key header
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with Idempotency-Key header, 5-day span
Assert HTTP 201 and capture request_id
POST identical request again with same Idempotency-Key
Assert HTTP 200 or 201 with same request_id (no duplicate created)
GET /leave-requests and assert only one record for the period
expected: Second POST returns same request_id as first POST
Only one leave request record exists for the date range
State transitions from draft to pending exactly once
trace: R-WEB-058: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_idempotent_under_retry(auth_session, clean_leave_state):
    import uuid
    key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    start = <next_business_day>(offset=1)
    end = <add_business_days>(start, 4)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "type": "annual"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": key}

    r1 = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    request_id = r1.json()["request_id"]

    r2 = auth_session.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert r2.json()["request_id"] == request_id

    all_requests = auth_session.get("/leave-requests", params={"start": start, "end": end}).json()
    matching = [r for r in all_requests if r["request_id"] == request_id]
    assert len(matching) == 1
